reasoning_content: We need to determine if 193 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. Check divisibility: 193 ends with 3, so not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 1+9+3=13, not divisible by 3. Not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*27=189, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: 11*17=187, remainder 6; 11*18=198 too high. 13: 13*14=182, remainder 11; 13*15=195 too high. 17: 17*11=187, remainder 6; 17*12=204 too high. 19: 19*10=190, remainder 3; 19*11=209 too high. Next prime 23: 23*8=184, remainder 9; 23*9=207 too high. 29: 29*6=174, remainder 19; 29*7=203 too high. 31: 31*6=186, remainder 7; 31*7=217 too high. 37: 37*5=185, remainder 8; 37*6=222 too high. 41: 41*4=164, remainder 29; 41*5=205 too high. 43: 43*4=172, remainder 21; 43*5=215 too high. Since sqrt(193) ≈ 13.89, we only need to check primes up to 13. Actually sqrt(193) is about 13.89, so we check primes up to 13: 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide evenly. So 193 is prime. Answer: yes.
